items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital spending plans
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on April 14 to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major infrastructure and facility projects the city plans to fund. This is worth following if you care about what physical improvements or developments Mountain View is prioritizing.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: The City Council introduced a new ordinance amending tenant protections in the Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.

items.headline: Youth services and library facilities plans reviewed
items.summary: The City Council reviewed progress on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports track investments in youth programs and public library improvements.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets density bonus for seismic upgrades
items.summary: A building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being elevated from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, allowing developers to add 5,000 square feet of floor area in exchange for seismic and historic rehabilitation work. This could signal how the city encourages preservation while enabling development.

items.headline: Figure AI expands San José office footprint
items.summary: The AI robotics company Figure AI is investing $4.1M in a second-floor office tenant improvement at 4000 N 1st Street, signaling continued growth of cutting-edge tech companies in the region.

items.headline: Major mixed-use project proposed near San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning request for 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road proposes an eight-story building with 167 residential units (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space, converting the site from commercial zoning to a new planned community district. This is a significant development that could reshape that corridor.

items.headline: Major tech company expansion at Automation Park
items.summary: A $7.4M tenant improvement project is underway at 1768 Automation Parkway, representing significant commercial investment in San José's tech corridor. This is one of the largest single commercial permits issued this period.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully visible in the agenda. Residents interested in upcoming construction projects may want to review the complete contract details.
